page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 3686 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 93241.53 and a PassMark CPU score of 76173.25. Memory bandwidth is highly capable, reaching 5034.94 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 324.54 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic operations are a notable strength, with the PassMark CPU encryption test placing in the top tier at 84840.45, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA-NI instruction sets. Single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1703.0.

This server is well-suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized compilation tasks, supported by a PassMark database score of 30836.72 and a Redis SET throughput of 86.05 million operations per second. While it handles smaller LLM inference tasks adequately, performance drops significantly on larger models, with 70B parameter text generation averaging a weak 2.73 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. The high resource density and substantial local storage capacity offer a balanced, cost-efficient profile for enterprise workloads requiring high CPU core counts and fast local disk access without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance includes 3686 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It delivers strong multi-core and memory performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 76173.25 and a small-block read memory bandwidth of 5034.94 GB/sec. Cryptographic execution is exceptionally strong, reaching top-tier levels in encryption benchmarks. While single-core performance and large-scale LLM inference are moderate to weak, the server excels in parallel processing, database operations, and high-throughput web serving. It offers a balanced, cost-efficient resource profile for enterprise workloads requiring substantial CPU capacity and local storage without the need for dedicated GPU hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-core workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs, substantial local storage, and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM
Includes 3686 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution
Features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for general-purpose workloads
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and parallelized compilation
